items.title: HUD issues FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) funding notice with key set-aside deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 implements FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding provisions, including $34.557 billion for HAP renewal funding and $2.836 billion for administrative fees. The notice also sets multiple application deadlines for HAP set-aside categories (many due June 26, 2026 by 5 p.m. local time) and notes the DocuSign application links go live June 1, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance outlines upcoming limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens (effective Oct. 1, 2026)
items.summary: CMS released guidance for states to implement statutory limits on claiming federal Medicaid and CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens, beginning October 1, 2026. CMS notes that federal matching funds for treatment of emergency medical conditions remain available, and the change does not apply to states’ options to cover lawfully residing children or pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: USDA announces reorganization and new "Food and Nutrition Administration" with SNAP leadership relocating to Indianapolis
items.summary: USDA announced plans to create a "Food and Nutrition Administration" and shift from regional offices to a hub model, with SNAP leadership relocating to Indianapolis, Indiana. USDA says the nutrition portfolio includes 16 programs and the reorganization is intended to improve service and oversight while continuing efforts to reduce fraud, waste, and abuse.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts: $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/partially breastfeeding participants. USDA also notes states must authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: IRS reminder: EITC refunds can’t be issued before Feb. 21, 2026; most eligible direct-deposit refunds expected by March 2
items.summary: The IRS says it must wait until at least February 21, 2026 to issue refunds to taxpayers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC). For taxpayers who file online, choose direct deposit, and have no issues with their return, the IRS expects most EITC-related refunds to be received by March 2, 2026.

items.title: Social Security announces 2.8% COLA for 2026, raising average monthly retirement benefit by about $56
items.summary: Social Security’s 2026 COLA is 2.8%, effective for benefits payable in January 2026. The SSA estimates the average monthly benefit for all retired workers will rise from $2,015 to $2,071 (an increase of $56) and the average for all disabled workers will rise from $1,586 to $1,630 (an increase of $44).

items.title: FAFSA deadline reminder: 2026-27 FAFSA due by June 30, 2027 (and 2025-26 FAFSA due by June 30, 2026)
items.summary: USA.gov lists the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2026-27 school year as June 30, 2027, and for the 2025-26 school year as June 30, 2026. Students still need to check state and school deadlines, which can be earlier.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: Starting in 2026-27, foreign earned income exclusion is added to AGI for max/min Pell eligibility
items.summary: Federal Student Aid guidance says that beginning with the 2026-27 award year, the foreign earned income exclusion amount reported on the FAFSA will be added to adjusted gross income (AGI) when determining eligibility for maximum and minimum Pell Grants. The guidance says this change was implemented with the official launch of the 2026-27 FAFSA form and means schools no longer need to use professional judgment to move the exclusion into AGI for Pell eligibility.
